aiComment: Six beds, three baths — the roomiest on your shortlist, and the guide eased from $900–990k three months ago. Closest to the Werribee vet (1.6km) and a 1.1km hop to Hoppers Crossing. Priced at the very top of your range, so only worth it if you genuinely need the space.

Five-bed at $860k, but the guide crept UP from a flat $860k two weeks ago — a confident seller. The catch: it's deep in the estate, 4.5km to Aircraft station and 3.8km to a Coles, the least connected of your top picks.

Five beds, three baths at $860k after a trim from $895–935k two months ago — real softening. Mid-estate: 2.7km to Hoppers Crossing, 2km to Coles. Strong space-for-money if the commute distance suits you.

Another five-bed/three-bath at $860k, with no advertised price movement to read into. Sits 3.1km from Aircraft station with two playgrounds close by — a family-friendly pocket, but you'll be driving to the train.
Right on your $850k target and cut from $890–930k two months ago. Best bit: a Coles just 695m away — the shortest grocery run on the list. Standard 4/2, 2.5km to Hoppers Crossing.
Bang on $850k and a 700m walk to Williams Landing — the best train access here. But it's already UNDER CONTRACT, so treat it as a sold comp rather than a live option unless the deal falls through.

On-target 4/2 at $850k with no price history to signal urgency. It's 3.4km from Aircraft station with no playgrounds within 500m — fine on paper, but nothing sets it apart from cheaper near-identical listings.

A textbook on-budget 4/2 at $850k, 3.1km from Aircraft station. No advertised price movement and no playgrounds nearby — a solid but unremarkable option that lives or dies on how the photos and floorplan stack up.

Five beds at $875k after a hefty cut from $945–995k two months ago — the steepest drop on your shortlist. That reads as a motivated seller. Aircraft station 2.9km; no playgrounds close, but the discount is the story.
On-target 4/2 at $850k, eased from $890–950k over three months — gentle, steady softening. Aircraft 3.2km away. Nothing dramatic, but a fair trend to negotiate against from a weakening guide.
Just under target at $849k, a 1km walk to Williams Landing and a Coles under 1km — one of the better-connected 4-bedders. Quiet court position. No price history, so no read on seller urgency.

My value pick: $849k, and the amenity trifecta — Coles 405m, Aircraft station under 1km, and four playgrounds within 500m (the most on your list). If the house itself checks out, this is the strongest all-rounder.
Seven beds, four baths — a genuinely big house — at $930k, guide nudged up recently. Williams Landing under 900m. Above your sweet spot; worth it only for a large or multi-generational household.

Five beds at $820k after a fresh cut just last week from $875–925k — the newest price drop here, so the seller may be chasing a deal. Aircraft 3km out. Best beds-per-dollar under budget if the distance is OK.
Under budget at $840k and trimmed from $885–935k last month. The downside is errands: 3.2km to the nearest Coles, one of the longer grocery runs. Decent price trend, weaker on day-to-day convenience.
